growth_records.strain: S106 (lowest-hydrolysis strain) and other C. amalonaticus isolates (species range 2-27%)

up.method: calculated: strain S106 = 2% (lowest of all isolates) and species range 2-27% of 0.05 g/100mL urea (=8.325 mmol/L) hydrolyzed over 24 h; rate = (fraction x 8.325 mmol/L)/24h

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Functional gene-guided enrichment plus in situ microsphere cultivation enables isolation of new crucial ureolytic bacteria from the rumen of cattle. Microbiome (2023). DOI:10.1186/s40168-023-01510-4. [PMC10105427]
growth_records.snippet: strain S106 (C. amalonaticus) the lowest urea hydrolysis...Strains of K. pneumoniae, C. koseri, C. amalonaticus, and A. butzleri hydrolyzed 20-54%, 11-42%, 2-27%, and 13-28%, respectively, of the urea added to the medium
growth_records.notes: Same assay/medium/formula as strain S99 record. S106 is explicitly named as the single lowest-hydrolysis isolate (2%); the 2-27% range given in text is for the C. amalonaticus species overall (multiple strains, exact n and per-strain values only in Fig. 6B, not in main text). Rate = (%hydrolyzed x 8.325 mmol/L urea)/24h. Volumetric, not gDW-normalized (see note in S99 record).
